quick_skim.title: Towards Federated Long-Tailed Graph Learning: An Energy-Guided Dual Decoupling Approach
quick_skim.abstract: Federated Graph Learning facilitates collaborative graph modeling across distributed clients while preserving data privacy. However, real-world data categories frequently exhibit long-tailed distributions. Such statistical scarcity severely degrades performance in two ways: it biases the global model toward majority classes, and it structurally isolates minority nodes by submerging them in heterophilic, head-dominated neighborhoods. While existing methods attempt topology-agnostic statistical compensations, they often fail under data scarcity. Instead of recovering tail nodes, they overfit the structural noise from adjacent dominant classes, leading to representation degradation. To address these limitations, we propose FedEPD, a framework built on a dual decoupling paradigm that separates topological purification from semantic recalibration. Specifically, FedEPD utilizes distribution-aware Dirichlet energy pruning to filter spatial heterophilic edges. It then overcomes Non-IID distribution shifts by extracting robust global prototypes from topologically central nodes, which are incorporated into local representations via a spatial low-pass prototype injection. Furthermore, a two stage alternating optimization strategy strictly protects majority decision boundaries while improving minority accuracy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FedEPD achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse long-tailed benchmarks, yielding absolute improvements of up to 4.97% in Accuracy and 5.48% in Macro-F1.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Federated Graph Learning for distributed training systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 联邦图学习作为分布式训练系统
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 联邦图学习作为分布式训练系统
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 联邦图学习作为分布式训练系统
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FedEPD for federated graph learning to handle long-tailed distributions via energy-guided dual decoupling, addressing privacy-preserving distributed training over graph data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对联邦图学习中的长尾分布问题，提出FedEPD方法，通过能量引导双解耦机制，在保护数据隐私的同时提升模型对尾部节点的表示能力。实验表明该方法在多个联邦图数据集上优于现有方法，为分布式图学习提供了有效解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对联邦图学习中的长尾分布问题，提出FedEPD方法，通过能量引导双解耦机制，在保护数据隐私的同时提升模型对尾部节点的表示能力。实验表明该方法在多个联邦图数据集上优于现有方法，为分布式图学习提供了有效解决方案。

quick_skim.title: Counting Triangles of Graphs via Randomized Trace Estimation with Incomplete Matrix-Vector Products
quick_skim.abstract: Counting triangles in graphs is a fundamental operation in network analysis, underpinning metrics such as clustering coefficients and serving as a signal for community detection, link prediction, and anomaly detection. The standard approach computes the trace of the cube of the adjacency matrix, but explicitly forming $\mathbf{A}^3$ is infeasible for large graphs. Hutchinson randomized trace estimator offers an efficient alternative by approximating the trace through stochastic averaging of quadratic forms, requiring only matrix vector products with $\mathbf{A}$. However, in distributed and heterogeneous computing environments, observing all entries of these products can be costly due to communication overhead and straggler effects. To address this, we propose a new variant of Hutchinson estimator that operates under partial observation constraints, where both the number and identities of observed entries are random. We provide theoretical guarantees on unbiasedness, variance bounds, and sample complexity, and demonstrate through experiments on synthetic and real world graphs that our method achieves accurate triangle count estimates while reducing synchronization costs. This work highlights the adaptability of randomized algorithms to modern computational architectures and opens avenues for efficient motif counting in large scale network analytics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Communication-efficient approach for distributed graph triangle counting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对分布式图三角形计数的通信高效方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对分布式图三角形计数的通信高效方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对分布式图三角形计数的通信高效方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a randomized trace estimation method with incomplete matrix-vector products to reduce communication overhead in distributed triangle counting, improving efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对分布式环境中图三角形计数的高通信开销问题，提出基于随机迹估计和不完全矩阵向量积的方法，通过减少完整矩阵向量积的通信需求，显著降低了分布式计算的通信成本。实验表明该方法在保证精度的同时大幅减少通信量，为大规模图分析提供高效的分布式算法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对分布式环境中图三角形计数的高通信开销问题，提出基于随机迹估计和不完全矩阵向量积的方法，通过减少完整矩阵向量积的通信需求，显著降低了分布式计算的通信成本。实验表明该方法在保证精度的同时大幅减少通信量，为大规模图分析提供高效的分布式算法。
